content: We can reframe human vocabulary learning of the word 'reflexion' (from the high school English curriculum finding) as an iterative self-refinement process analogous to how LLM self-refinement agents work: a learner's initial incorrect guess of the word's meaning or spelling becomes more accurate through repeated self-reflexive check, just as an LLM's raw output gets polished through structured self-feedback cycles. This turns passive vocabulary memorization into an autonomous, agent-like iterative improvement practice for language learners.
